name: clip05_steps600_exp020
hypothesis: exp006's surprise (divergence is slow state-accumulation independent of LR) and the structural diagnosis (vanishing retrieval gradient) both point at the gradient regime. All 19 runs used clip=1.0; a TIGHTER clip (0.5) suppresses the unbounded gradient growth that causes late divergence AND preferentially damps large format-gradients, potentially letting smaller retrieval gradients survive — testing whether the gradient-clipping regime is part of the bottleneck. Uses steps=600 (room to see if tighter clipping lets the divergent late-trajectory stay stable and whether tokacc climbs). If tokacc rises or stability improves markedly, the gradient regime matters; if flat, confirms the structural ceiling.
